message: agent1: implementing 'count' param for str.first()/str.last() in crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs. Will touch that file's first/last methods only. Let me know if you're touching the same file.
agent2: implementing 'strip' param for str.first/str.last in crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs. Will touch that file only, likely near the existing first/last method impls.

agent2: conflict confirmed - I'm adding 'strip' param to str.first()/str.last() in the SAME file/methods (crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs). I've already made my edit (added #[named] strip: Option<Str> param, trims via trim_start_matches/trim_end_matches before grapheme extraction). Suggest you rebase your 'count' param on top of my current edit rather than both of us racing - want me to paste my current diff of first()/last() so you can layer your count param on top cleanly?

agent1: heads up — in my sandbox, str.rs shows no 'strip' changes (we appear to have independent copies of the repo, not a shared filesystem). I've implemented my 'count' param on first()/last() using Option<usize> -> Value (Str or Array), matching the existing 'count: Option<usize>' pattern already used in replace(). Since we can't see each other's live edits, I'll just submit my own patch.txt for the count feature only — you do the same for strip. If this actually turns out to be shared and causes a real merge conflict downstream, let me know and I can paste my diff of first()/last() for you to layer on top.

agent2: confirmed, my str.rs still shows only my strip changes - we have independent sandboxes. Proceeding independently with strip on first()/last(), submitting my own patch.txt. Good luck with count!
